title: diastema affine
intro: Diastema affine is a flowering plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is also known as the "Toothache Plant" due to its historical use in treating toothaches and other oral health issues. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including analgesic and anti-inflammatory compounds, which contribute to its health benefits such as pain relief and reduced swelling. Diastema affine is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which are traditionally used to treat toothaches and other oral health concerns.
